errors.message:

warnings:
git clone failed: Cloning into '/Users/ghh/Documents/Code/Code2MCP-private/workspace/poliastro/temp_clone'...
fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/poliastro/poliastro/': LibreSSL SSL_connect: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL in connection to github.com:443
recommendations: add and run an MCP smoke/integration test suite (current test status is empty) that validates each generated endpoint (`orbit`
propagators
`maneuver`
`body`
`ephem`
`lambert`
`orbitplotter`) end-to-end
deduplicate the two `lambert` endpoints by renaming to method-specific tools (e.g.
`lambert_izzo`
`lambert_vallado`) to avoid API ambiguity
improve packaging detection/build metadata in the importer (analysis incorrectly reports missing `pyproject.toml` despite it existing) to make dependency resolution reliable
pin and align MCP plugin/runtime dependencies with the upstream project (numpy/astropy/scipy compatibility matrix + optional extras for plotting) and document supported versions
add strict input/output schemas for astropy quantities and time types in the adapter (units
frames
epochs) with clear serialization rules to prevent runtime conversion errors
implement robust error mapping in MCP service (domain exceptions -> structured MCP errors with actionable messages)
create a minimal “golden examples” test set from existing docs notebooks to verify MCP outputs against known orbit/lambert/propagation results
add CI workflow for `mcp_output` (lint
type-check
tests
startup check) and publish artifacts/health status badges in `README_MCP.md`
add lightweight performance benchmarks for heavy paths (Cowell propagation
Lambert solves
plotting data prep) with regression thresholds
strengthen observability by adding structured logging and per-endpoint timing/failed-call metrics
reduce surface-area risk by exposing a curated stable endpoint layer instead of direct class-level mirrors where possible
add contract tests for adapter mode `import` plus fallback behavior to ensure resilience when optional dependencies are missing
improve documentation with endpoint-by-endpoint request/response examples and common failure cases (units mismatch
invalid epochs
unavailable ephemerides)
add security hardening for MCP server execution (input size limits
timeout guards for long propagations
safe defaults)
track technical debt in `contrib/` by explicitly excluding non-production modules from MCP auto-exposure and documenting rationale

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Zip fallback ingestion succeeded after git clone failure, allowing repository analysis (244 files discovered).
Import-mode adapter strategy was feasible (import feasibility 0.95) for a Python library with low intrusiveness risk.
MCP plugin startup/health checks passed (service healthy over stdio).
Core endpoint surface was generated for major poliastro domains (orbit, propagators, maneuvers, bodies, ephemerides, Lambert, plotting).
failure_reasons: Primary source acquisition failed at DownloadNode due to TLS/network error (LibreSSL SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL to github.com:443).
Dependency/packaging detection is incorrect (reported no pyproject/requirements despite pyproject.toml present), reducing build reliability.
Validation quality is weak: original project tests did not run (passed=false, empty details) and MCP integration tests are effectively absent.
Generated metadata is inconsistent (total LOC/files size/tool endpoint counts reported as 0 while files/endpoints exist).
DeepWiki augmentation failed, reducing analysis depth.

node_performance.download_time: Download path partially failed (git clone), then recovered via zip fallback; reliability concern outweighs speed.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with broad repository scan; detected structure and core modules, but packaging inference errors indicate parser quality gaps.
node_performance.generation_time: Generated MCP scaffold and endpoint set successfully; however duplicate lambert endpoint and zeroed generation metrics indicate post-processing/reporting defects.
node_performance.test_time: MCP health test passed quickly, but no meaningful endpoint-level or regression test execution; test confidence is low.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: No usable telemetry (reported 0 MB); cannot validate efficiency. Add runtime instrumentation.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: No usable telemetry (reported 0%); profiling data missing.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Generated artifacts are small scaffold files, but reported size=0 is inconsistent; disk accounting is currently unreliable.
